flags.concern: The resize step drops the repo's current live-update path. Today the editor relies on row updates during resize, while `useClipResize` only does start/end bookkeeping and final persistence. A custom pointer handler that only fires `onActionResizeStart`/`onActionResizeEnd` will not visibly resize clips unless `TimelineCanvas` also owns transient action geometry or drives `onChange` during drag.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts:62-65` documents `onChange` as the resize-driven row update path. `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts:12-15` exposes only start/end handlers, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts:36-89` commits the final row/meta update only on resize end. There is no existing consumer for an `onActionResizing` callback in this repo.

flags.concern: The plan explicitly defers virtualization even though the stated usage includes 50+ tracks, and it does not call out vertical scroll syncing between the track-label column and the timeline body. In this repo those are separate panes, so a naive native-scroll replacement is likely to regress both performance and row/label alignment on large timelines.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:179-223` renders the left track labels and right timeline as separate containers. `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TrackLabel.tsx:30-86` renders one DOM row per track. `src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts:60-86` currently depends on the library grid's scroll container (`.ReactVirtualized__Grid`), which shows the existing implementation already assumes a dedicated scrolling surface.

flags.concern: The type-replacement step is incomplete for the stated goal of removing all `@xzdarcy` imports. The repo still pulls `TimelineEffect` from `@xzdarcy/timeline-engine`, so replacing only `TimelineAction`/`TimelineRow`/the ref handle will leave either a broken import or an orphaned `effects` field.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:1-6` imports `TimelineEffect as EditorTimelineEffect`, and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:60-67` keeps it on `TimelineData.effects`. The proposed local type file only covers `TimelineAction`, `TimelineRow`, and `TimelineCanvasHandle`.
